Mensural notation Mensural notation is the musical notation system used for European vocal polyphonic music from the later part of the 13th century until about 1600. Its modern name is inspired by the terminology of medieval theorists, who used terms like ""musica mensurata"" (""measured music"") or ""cantus mensurabilis"" (""measurable song"") to refer to the rhythmically defined polyphonic music of their age, as opposed to ""musica plana"" or ""musica choralis"", i.e., Gregorian plainchant.
